original_image_tags: The page appears to be the reverse side of a carbon copy or a thin, translucent document (like onion skin paper), with text from the other side bleeding through and appearing as a mirror image. The page is light blue/grey, worn, with several punched holes at the top and a torn corner at the top right. There are several stamps visible on this side of the page.
A blue stamp, partially obscured and at an angle, reads "RECEIVED OCT 3 4 26 PM" and below that, "INTERNAL SECURITY - F.B.I."

elements.description: Faded, semi-legible typed text visible on the paper, with some reversed text due to bleed-through. Two blue rubber stamps are visible at the bottom: one inverted reading 'RECEIVED INTERNAL SECURITY F.B.I. OCT 3 4 26 PM', and one reading 'RECEIVED MAIL ROOM F.B.I. SEP 28 1947'. A rectangular purple stamp reading 'RECORDS DIVISION' is also present at the bottom right.

judge.reason: Description B claims a 'RECEIVED MAIL ROOM F.B.I. SEP 28 1947' stamp and a 'RECORDS DIVISION' stamp, which are specific visual elements entirely absent from Description A, which only mentions one stamp.
